items.title: The Great British Takeover Tops $60 Billion After Mitie Deal
items.description: The UK stock market is losing the equivalent of more than $2 billion a week to takeovers of London-listed companies, with Mitie Group Plc the latest name to be snapped up.

items.title: Danantara Hires Banks for Planned Second Dollar Bond Sale
items.description: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund Danantara has mandated banks to sound out investor demand for a potential sale of long-dated dollar bonds, weeks after completing its first offshore debt offering.

items.title: Zalando Avoids Fine as German Regulator Ends Disclosure Probe
items.description: Germany’s financial regulator concluded a review into Zalando SE over a missing disclosure in its annual report without imposing a fine.

items.title: Millikin University Sweetens Bond Deal By Adding 600-Bed Student Apartments
items.description: A private college in Illinois is sweetening its upcoming bond deal by adding a student apartment complex to increase investor protections.

items.title: HSBC’s Kettner Sees Risk of Pullback in Stocks Before Midterms
items.description: Equity investors should look to reduce some exposure after this earnings season, according to HSBC Holdings Plc’s Max Kettner, who warns that stretched sentiment, a fading fiscal impulse, and US midte

items.title: Gilts Climb as Investors Await More Policy Details From Burnham
items.description: UK government bonds posted small moves as investors awaited fresh policy details from new Prime Minister Andy Burnham, while weak economic data dimmed bets on higher interest rates.

items.title: London Stock Exchange to Introduce Nonstop Trading Next Year
items.description: The London Stock Exchange will open a new venue outside of its regular market hours, a move to offer “near-continuous trading” and better compete with the 24/7 trading offered by alternative platforms

items.title: Indonesia Markets First Panda Bonds as It Broadens Funding
items.description: Indonesia has begun marketing its inaugural yuan-denominated bonds in China’s domestic debt market, as Southeast Asia’s largest economy seeks to diversify funding sources amid currency volatility and

items.title: Iran War Forces Cash-Strapped Asian Nations to Buy Expensive LNG
items.description: Pakistan and Bangladesh were forced to buy some of their most expensive liquefied natural gas shipments in years as the Middle East conflict chokes supply, straining government finances and prompting

items.title: Another Ship Hit in Hormuz as Houthis Add to Regional Risks
items.description: Another tanker was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz as renewed hostilities empty the waterway, while a threat by Houthi rebels to blockade Saudi Arabia in the Red Sea heightened regional maritime risk

items.title: FTSE 100 Live: Pound, Gilts Steady After Healey Named Chancellor
Palm Slips from Month-High as Weak Crude Dents Biofuel Appeal
items.description: Palm oil fell from a near one-month high following weakness in the crude and vegetable oil complex, denting the appeal of biofuels.

items.title: Japan Insurers Buy Most Super-Long Bonds in Three Years in June
items.description: Japanese insurance firms bought the most super-long government bonds in three years last month, adding to signs that demand from a key buyer is stabilizing.

items.title: Iron Ore Drops as China Steel Losses Deepen, Hormuz Risks Rise
items.description: Iron ore fell for a second day as deteriorating profit margins at Chinese steel mills and continued US-Iran hostilities clouded the demand outlook.

items.title: Goldman Says Brent Could Top $120 If Hormuz Disruptions Persist
items.description: Brent could rally to more than $120 a barrel by the fourth quarter if disruptions of the Strait of Hormuz persist, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc., although that’s not the bank’s base case.

items.title: China Unleashes Broad State Support to Halt Tech Stock Selloff
items.description: China is mounting one of its broadest efforts in years to steady the stock market, with regulators, state-backed investors, insurers and asset managers all moving to shore up confidence after a sellof

items.title: SBI Funds Rises 8.5% in Debut After $1 Billion India IPO
items.description: SBI Funds Management Ltd. shares rose 8.5% in their Mumbai debut Tuesday after investors piled into the company’s $1 billion initial public offering, making it one of India’s most heavily subscribed b

items.title: Gold Rises on Dip-Buying as Traders Weigh Middle East Clashes
items.description: Gold touched its highest level in a week on dip-buying as traders monitored developments in the Middle East for clues on the energy price impact on inflation.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
items.description: Brent oil swung after rallying more than 20% this month, as traders weighed threats to maritime traffic from the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea to a key Kazakh export terminal on Russia’s coast.

items.title: Stocks Rise as Rebound in Chipmakers Gathers Pace: Markets Wrap
items.description: A rebound in chipmakers gathered pace on Tuesday, driving stock markets higher in an otherwise quiet session that saw modest moves in Treasury yields, oil and currencies.
